function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'fclose' to reach function 'fopen'?

loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < ROWS' is never executed?

Is it possible that the loop with condition 'j < COLS' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'j < COLS' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 8:  <statement> for (int i = 0; i < ROWS; ++i) </statement> is reached, the statement line 27:  <statement> fclose(wrkFile); </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 23:  <statement> fprintf(wrkFile, "%7.1f", vals[i][j]); </statement> dominate line 25:  <statement> fprintf(wrkFile, "\n"); </statement>?
data_dependency: Does variable j at line 10: <statement> 		for (int j = 0; j < COLS; ++j) </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 19: <statement> 	for (int i = 0; i < ROWS; ++i) </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'i' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'vals' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Is the statement 'for (int j = 0; j < COLS; ++j)' unreachable?
